theme: the org Workforce-Intelligence dashboard surfaces (Must #2) — make every dashboard aggregate render believably (non-empty + distributed, not binary-or-zero) for a seeded story. Six new seeders land the dashboard spine: membership_skills (the mapped->verified verification funnel, joined on skill NAME not node-id, mapped outnumbering verified per skill); tags/membership_tags (a dozen business-unit teams + a cross-cutting mentor tag); organization_target_roles+user_target_roles (the role gap + two-way internal mobility); succession (interview_extraction_results for >20% of members to clear the coverage gate too_sparse->full); feedback (job_simulation_feedbacks ~2:1 positive, Italgas-shaped); population_evidence (the org-scale claimed-vs-verified gap + AI-readiness skills). Plus two fixes: the assignments status-mix (completed/overdue/in-progress/not-started via status + due_date + an organization_assignment_sessions row on the skill-path FK arm, writing local_skill_path_sessions) and the skillpath_sessions completed-share bump (~1% -> ~30%). The seed-side closure gene now spans four skill-ref surfaces (adds membership_skills.skill_id). Tooling + docs only — zero platform-repo edits.

coverage.note: seeders dipped from M35's 98.0% because M36 added 6 new seeders (a large new statement surface) — the harden passes recovered it to 95.5%. The 6 new seeders' load-bearing distribution helpers, the skillref_named resolver, and assignments.go (the FK-arm chain) are all at 100%; residual sub-100% is uniform defensive registration accessors + a couple of no-pool guards (no behaviour to probe — shallow box-ticking declined). dna 87.7% (the closure-gene extension is exercised; residual is CLI-glue).

benchmarks: n/a — no performance-sensitive hot path; the seeders are DB-IO-bound bulk COPY/UPSERT, no benchmark target.
flake_gate: PASS — 5/5 sequential shuffled (-shuffle=<random>) -race runs of the full ./... suite, 0 flakes. Deterministic by construction (seeded hashes via deterministicUUID/hashInt, no time/random in the seed path).

alignment_gates: 100%/100% on all 4 Clerkenstein surfaces — UNTOUCHED by M36 (M36 is stack-seeding only; no clerkenstein code change). The multi-identity seat-switch is M37.
review_summary: close GREEN — 4 findings, 0 blocking. Tests (1): the README handbook test-count reconciliation (same fix as the doc gap); dna README's 117 already accurate. done_bar: MET (orchestrator-verified LIVE on the --local-content demo-3 stack, Cervato org): every dashboard aggregate is populated + distributed — the verification funnel (220 mapped members / 484 mapped skills -> believable drop-off to verified), the claimed-vs-verified self-eval gap (172 over-claimers / 137 under-claimers), 13 teams (incl. mentor), 70 org target roles, succession 29% interviewed (clears the >20% gate -> 'full'), feedback 2.3:1 positive, assignments 70 active / 40 completed (real status mix). The build's integration test (TestIntegration_DashboardSurfaces, opt-in) independently confirms every aggregate FUNCTION resolves. Unlike M34/M35, M36's dashboard is the ADMIN view — viewable by the demo login user (a Cervato admin) WITHOUT M37, so the browser render is achievable now (the data acceptance is already met).
notes: Third milestone of v1.9 (M34 + M35 done, M36 done, M37->M38 remain). Both product Musts are now delivered (M34 the individual skill profile, M36 the org Workforce dashboard). The natural next step is M37 (Clerkenstein multi-identity), which may run alongside M36 but is now next in sequence. M36's data layer + M35's Stories engine are what the cockpit (M37/M38) drives.
